name: Raw KLF-200 API: send a request, and listen for live notifications
info:
The Velux Api node has two independent uses:

**1. Request / response** - send a message in to call an API command. The command
is taken from `msg.payload.api` (numeric) or `msg.payload.apiText` (name); if the
payload carries neither, the command configured on the node is used. The gateway's
answer leaves the node's output.

**2. Notifications** - select one or more notification types in the `ntf` list and
the node emits every matching frame the gateway pushes, without any input.
`529` (0x0211) is `GW_NODE_STATE_POSITION_CHANGED_NTF`, which fires whenever a
product moves - including movements triggered by io-homecontrol hand-held remotes.

Note: notifications are only pushed when the connection node has the house status
monitor enabled (`monitor: MONITOR`).

The lower node is notification-only - it has no input wire, so its selected command
is never sent. A command still has to be selected because the field is mandatory.
